steps.title: Recall the 30-60-90 triangle side ratio
steps.content:
In a 30-60-90 triangle, the sides opposite the 30°, 60°, and 90° angles are always in the ratio 1: √3: 2. If we let the shortest side (opposite 30°) be $s$, then the three sides are:
  • Shortest side (opposite 30°): $s$
  • Middle side (opposite 60°): $s\sqrt{3}$
  • Longest side, the hypotenuse (opposite 90°): $2s$

steps.title: Apply the perimeter condition
steps.content:
The perimeter is the sum of all three sides:
$$s + s\sqrt{3} + 2s = 18 + 6\sqrt{3}$$
Combine like terms on the left:
$$s(1 + \sqrt{3} + 2) = 18 + 6\sqrt{3}$$
$$s(3 + \sqrt{3}) = 18 + 6\sqrt{3}$$
Notice that the right side factors as $6(3 + \sqrt{3})$.

steps.title: Solve for the scale factor and find the longest side
steps.content:
Since $s(3 + \sqrt{3}) = 6(3 + \sqrt{3})$, divide both sides by $(3 + \sqrt{3})$:
$$s = 6$$
The longest side is the hypotenuse, which equals $2s = 2(6) = 12$.
